code: @Manual{,   title = {gp3ml: Leakage-Safe Predictive Validation for Gazepoint Research},   author = {Stefanos Balaskas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://stefanosbalaskas.github.io/gp3ml/}, }

title: gp3ml
text: gp3ml R package cautious development leakage-resistant group-aware predictive validation using Gazepoint-derived research data.

title: gp3ml
text: first development stages focus : participant-, trial-, stimulus-leakage auditing; explicit generalization-target declarations; outcome, predictor, identifier-role validation; feature-provenance manifests; grouped train/test splitting; grouped nested resampling plans; fold-overlap balance diagnostics; calibration uncertainty assessment; model-card reproducibility reporting. package intended generic wrapper around existing machine-learning frameworks.

title: gp3ml
text: Participant overlap treated failure declared target requires generalization new participants. Stimulus overlap treated failure declared target requires generalization unseen stimuli. Preprocessing feature selection must estimated inside relevant resampling folds. package examples tests use deterministic synthetic data.

title: gp3ml
text: audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage() audits already-defined analysis assessment partitions predictive evaluation. checks: compatibility declared generalization target; participant, participant-trial, stimulus overlap; outcome declared identifiers included predictors; declared target-derived post-outcome predictors; exact row overlap repeated predictor profiles; duplicated rows missing grouping identifiers; identifier-like predictor names requiring manual review. returned audit overall pass, review, fail status, complete check table, non-passing issue table, partition summaries. Audit tables can exported using write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv(). audit identifies structural risks visible supplied partitions declared variable roles. establish preprocessing feature selection estimated within resampling folds; safeguards require separate provenance resampling infrastructure.
code: analysis <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P01", "P02"),   trial_id = c("T01", "T02"),   stimulus_id = c("S01", "S02"),   outcome = c(0, 1),   fixation_duration = c(210, 245) )  assessment <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P03", "P04"),   trial_id = c("T03", "T04"),   stimulus_id = c("S03", "S04"),   outcome = c(1, 0),   fixation_duration = c(275, 230) )  audit <- audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage(   analysis = analysis,   assessment = assessment,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = "fixation_duration",   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   stimulus_id = "stimulus_id",   generalization_target = "new_participants" )  audit

title: gp3ml
text: package support person identification, health inference, protected-attribute prediction, direct inference emotion, stress, mental state, cognition, comprehension, personality, deception, intent. See: GOVERNANCE.md PROHIBITED-USE.md

title: gp3ml
text: Version 0.0.0.9000 now includes first governance feature: structured leakage auditing already-defined analysis assessment partitions, together machine-readable CSV export. model-training interface, automated feature selection, resampling engine implemented.

what: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
text: Audits already-defined analysis assessment partitions common forms leakage incompatibility declared generalization target. function create data splits, preprocess variables, select features, fit predictive models.

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
code: audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage(   analysis,   assessment,   outcome,   predictors,   participant_id = NULL,   trial_id = NULL,   stimulus_id = NULL,   generalization_target = c("new_trials_known_participants", "new_participants",     "new_stimuli", "new_participants_and_new_stimuli"),   target_derived = character(),   post_outcome = character() )

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
text: analysis data frame containing analysis training partition. assessment data frame containing assessment test partition. outcome single column name identifying outcome. predictors character vector identifying intended predictor columns. participant_id optional participant-identifier column. trial_id optional trial-identifier column. stimulus_id optional stimulus-identifier column. generalization_target predictive generalization target. One "new_trials_known_participants", "new_participants", "new_stimuli", "new_participants_and_new_stimuli". target_derived Character vector columns known derived directly outcome. post_outcome Character vector columns measured constructed outcome became available.

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
text: object class gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit. object contains overall status, partition summary, complete check table, machine-readable table non-passing issues.

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
text: overall status "fail" least one failing check present, "review" failing checks present least one review item present, "pass" otherwise. participant_id supplied, trial overlap evaluated using composite participant-trial units. permits trial labels "T01" reused different participants without treated leakage. Without participant_id, trial_id assumed globally unique. audit can identify structural leakage visible supplied partitions declared variable roles. prove preprocessing feature selection estimated inside resampling folds. operations require separate provenance resampling safeguards. function determine whether outcome scientifically ethically appropriate. uses remain subject package governance prohibited-use statements.

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
code: analysis <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P01", "P01", "P02", "P02"),   trial_id = c("T01", "T02", "T03", "T04"),   stimulus_id = c("S01", "S02", "S03", "S04"),   outcome = c(0, 1, 0, 1),   fixation_duration = c(210, 240, 225, 260),   pupil_change = c(0.10, 0.16, 0.12, 0.18) )  assessment <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P03", "P03", "P04", "P04"),   trial_id = c("T05", "T06", "T07", "T08"),   stimulus_id = c("S05", "S06", "S07", "S08"),   outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0),   fixation_duration = c(275, 230, 290, 245),   pupil_change = c(0.21, 0.11, 0.24, 0.14) )  audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage(   analysis = analysis,   assessment = assessment,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   stimulus_id = "stimulus_id",   generalization_target = "new_participants" ) #> <gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit> #> Overall status: PASS #> Generalization target: new_participants #> Rows: 4 analysis; 4 assessment #> Non-passing checks: 0 #> No leakage issues were detected by the implemented checks.

what: Write a Gazepoint ML leakage-audit table to CSV — write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv
title: Write a Gazepoint ML leakage-audit table to CSV — write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv
text: Writes one machine-readable table leakage-audit object UTF-8 CSV file. Existing files replaced unless explicitly permitted.

title: Write a Gazepoint ML leakage-audit table to CSV — write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv
code: write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv(   x,   file,   table = c("issues", "checks", "partition_summary"),   overwrite = FALSE,   na = "" )

title: Write a Gazepoint ML leakage-audit table to CSV — write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv
text: x object returned audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage(). file single output file path ending .csv. table audit table export. One "issues", "checks", "partition_summary". overwrite Logical. FALSE, default, existing output file causes error. na Character value used missing values CSV file.

title: Write a Gazepoint ML leakage-audit table to CSV — write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv
text: normalized output path, invisibly.

title: Write a Gazepoint ML leakage-audit table to CSV — write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv
code: analysis <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P01", "P02"),   trial_id = c("T01", "T02"),   outcome = c(0, 1),   feature = c(1.2, 1.8) )  assessment <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P03", "P04"),   trial_id = c("T03", "T04"),   outcome = c(1, 0),   feature = c(2.1, 2.4) )  audit <- audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage(   analysis = analysis,   assessment = assessment,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = "feature",   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   generalization_target = "new_participants" )  output <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")  write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv(   audit,   output,   table = "checks" )  unlink(output)

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Added audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage() structured auditing already-defined analysis assessment partitions. Added explicit checks participant, participant-trial, stimulus compatibility declared generalization targets. Added checks exact-row overlap, duplicated rows, repeated predictor profiles, identifier predictors, target-derived predictors, post-outcome predictors. Added pass, review, fail audit statuses complete check, issue, partition-summary tables. Added write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv() machine-readable audit export. Added deterministic synthetic tests covering clean intentionally contaminated partitions.

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Created minimal R package structure. Added explicit model-governance prohibited-use statements. Restricted initial scope validation governance. Confirmed examples tests use deterministic synthetic data . model-training functions implemented.
